channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Comedian Sheng Wang on finding joy in the mundane
item.description: In his new Netflix special, comedian Sheng Wang elevates mundane moments - like picking a toothbrush - into punchlines. NPR's Emily Feng talks to the comedian about where he finds joy.
item.content:encoded: In his new Netflix special, comedian Sheng Wang elevates mundane moments - like picking a toothbrush - into punchlines. NPR's Emily Feng talks to the comedian about where he finds joy.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Oil and gas companies making hay by making plastic?
item.description: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.content:encoded: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: An inevitable rise in Chinese-made EVs in America?
item.description: Chinese-made EVs that are not currently legally sold in the U.S. are making their way across the border anyway, says Wall Street Journal reporter Ryan Felton, who recently covered a rise in interest in the vehicles.
item.content:encoded: Chinese-made EVs that are not currently legally sold in the U.S. are making their way across the border anyway, says Wall Street Journal reporter Ryan Felton, who recently covered a rise in interest in the vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Is there a 'standard' Indian body?
item.description: India recently published a survey to 'standardize' the Indian body sizes, so its manufacturers don't have to use the U.S. and E.U. sizes for a billion people. But is there a 'standard' Indian body?
item.content:encoded: India recently published a survey to 'standardize' the Indian body sizes, so its manufacturers don't have to use the U.S. and E.U. sizes for a billion people. But is there a 'standard' Indian body?
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: Collaboration helps NPR journalists get you the story of the Iran war up close
item.description: Three NPR journalists talk about the challenge of producing independent, accurate coverage of the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Three NPR journalists talk about the challenge of producing independent, accurate coverage of the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: Justice Alito and the Voting Rights Act
item.description: NPR's Emily Feng speaks with historian Peter Canellos about the Supreme Court's recent voting rights decision and Justice Samuel Alito's role in it.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Emily Feng speaks with historian Peter Canellos about the Supreme Court's recent voting rights decision and Justice Samuel Alito's role in it.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: ICRC President Mirjana Spoljaric warns of the war's impact on civilians
item.description: The ICRC president talks about her recent trip to Iran, and warns about the impact of the Iran war on civilians.
item.content:encoded: The ICRC president talks about her recent trip to Iran, and warns about the impact of the Iran war on civilians.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: An appeals court blocks telemedicine access to an abortion pill nationally, for now
item.description: A panel of judges in Louisiana has just ended telemedicine access to the abortion pill mifepristone nationally.
item.content:encoded: A panel of judges in Louisiana has just ended telemedicine access to the abortion pill mifepristone nationally.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: AI robot tech bros dogs run wild in Berlin gallery
item.description: In Germany, robotic AI dogs with the faces of tech's most powerful men are on the loose — courtesy of American artist Beeple.
item.content:encoded: In Germany, robotic AI dogs with the faces of tech's most powerful men are on the loose — courtesy of American artist Beeple.
item.dc:creator: Esme Nicholson

item.title: Iran war shakes Global economy as energy costs surge and recession fears grow
item.description: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.content:encoded: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer
